engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor decides by browsing menu, prices, photos, locations, hours, and directions — checking a physical eatery. No expertise sold, no portfolio; the place (food + venue) is the decision.

goal.one_action: reserve a table or order takeaway from one of the two restaurants

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-location restaurant site: home intro + philosophy, full menu, takeaway menu, reservation page, locations page. All transactions handed off to third-party platforms.

mapped.observed_as: Angello, pizzerias & restaurants italiens à Rennes
Nous fabriquons notre pâte à pizza et nos pâtes fraîches sur place avec une farine biologique en provenance d'Italie
Des produits frais / La tradition italienne / L'art de la panification
Formules / Antipasti / Pâtes fraîches / Pizzas / Desserts (menu with prices)
Allergènes / Les Traductions (allergen list + menu glossary)
Chez Angello (ordering channels: online, phone, delivery)
Champion de France de pizza 2014 + TripAdvisor

unmapped.observed: Formules / menu-list sections (structured multi-category menu with size-tiered prices)
unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical menu section id; mapped to 'pricing' as closest but a restaurant menu is a distinct component, not a SaaS price table

proposed_new.name: external-order-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Every takeaway/online-order CTA hands off to a third-party food-ordering platform (angello.toctok.fr, Uber Eats, Les Coursiers Rennais); no native order form or cart — the food-ordering analogue of external-booking-redirect (which covers the 1001menus table-reservation handoff here).

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full; all pages, menus, CTAs, ordering channels, and proof captured clearly.

gap_findings: No canonical 'menu' section id — restaurant menus (categorized, size/price tiered) are forced into 'pricing', a poor fit for the place engine.
Two distinct external-handoff patterns coexist: table reservation (external-booking-redirect) and food ordering (external-order-redirect) — vocab currently only has the booking side.
Multi-location single-brand (two venues with different hours/phones/ordering setups) has no clean structural expression beyond a location-card grid.
